context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T22:38Z after Astro-first UI architecture and first custom-element island slice. Branch develop is intentionally local-ahead of origin/develop by 16 commits because user requested batching local work instead of pushing/watching GitHub Actions after each small slice. Worktree should be clean after this handoff commit. Disk remains constrained but stable at ~13GB free on /workspaces/refarm; no cleanup was necessary.
current_tasks: Latest remote-pushed/CI-green baseline remains 4e02e548 `refactor(homestead): scope surface context providers` unless pushed later.

Local commits ahead of origin/develop now include the prior Homestead/plugin surface action batch plus new UI architecture commits: c3cb4a2e `feat(app-dev): prove surface action telemetry`; 4b01e422 `feat(homestead): compose surface host contracts`; ff8918eb `docs(plugins): document surface action contract`; 25834a7b `docs(ui): define astro-first architecture`; b0f73faa `refactor(app-dev): mount surface ledger as custom element`; b0b98670 `docs(ui): checkpoint ledger island boundary`.

25834a7b added `docs/UI_ARCHITECTURE.md` with the accepted policy: Astro owns stable page/layout composition; Homestead owns runtime/surface contracts; live runtime state should use small controllers/custom elements; plugin-rendered HTML remains a trust-gated Homestead surface boundary. `docs/EXTENSIBILITY_MODEL.md` links to it.

b0f73faa introduced a custom-element boundary for the `/surfaces` ledger: `STUDIO_SURFACE_LEDGER_ELEMENT_NAME`, `defineStudioSurfaceLedgerElement(...)`, `mountReactiveStudioSurfaceLedgerElement(...)`, `StudioSurfaceLedgerMountOptions`, and `StudioSurfaceLedgerElement` in `apps/dev/src/lib/surface-ledger.ts`. `apps/dev/src/pages/surfaces.astro` now renders `<refarm-surface-ledger ...>` as stable Astro markup and wires it with the runtime helper after boot.

b0b98670 updated `docs/UI_ARCHITECTURE.md` to note that `surface-ledger.ts` has an island/custom-element boundary but should not keep growing as an unbounded DOM renderer. Future work should push stable table/copy structure toward Astro or another shared primitive.

Previous slice details still matter: Homestead SDK has action telemetry helpers in `surface-inspector`, action/context compose helpers in `surface-renderer`, `/surfaces` can trigger requested/failed action telemetry, and plugin docs/examples now document `request.host.actions` plus `data-refarm-surface-action-id`.

Validation passed for new UI/custom-element work: `npm --prefix apps/dev test -- surface-ledger.test.ts`; `npm --prefix apps/dev run type-check`; `npm --prefix apps/dev run build`; `npm run project:validate --silent`; `git diff --check`. Earlier in the same turn also passed Homestead/app validations for surface action composition and plugin example smoke commands.
Known Vite browser-externalization warnings appeared during apps/dev build for `node:fs/promises` and `node:path`; unchanged/non-blocking. No sqlite benchmark worker diff appeared after builds.

blockers: Do not push/watch GitHub Actions after every small slice. User preference remains: validate locally, accumulate meaningful package, then push/monitor CI once.
